questions.text: The rate of flow of electric charge through any cross-section of a conductor is called:
questions.explanation: I = q / t. The SI unit is Ampere.

questions.text: Which of the following is the SI unit of electric current?
questions.explanation: One Ampere is the flow of one Coulomb of charge per second.

questions.text: The average velocity with which free electrons in a conductor get drifted towards the positive terminal under the influence of an electric field is:
questions.explanation: Drift velocity (vd) is typically of the order of 10^-4 m/s.

questions.text: Ohm's law relates:
questions.explanation: V = IR, stating that current through a conductor is directly proportional to potential difference across it.

questions.text: The resistance of a conductor is directly proportional to its ________ and inversely proportional to its ________.
questions.explanation: R = ρ (L / A).

questions.text: The reciprocal of resistance is called:
questions.explanation: G = 1/R. Unit is mho or Siemens.

questions.text: The SI unit of resistivity is:
questions.explanation: From R = ρL/A, ρ = RA/L. Units: Ohm * m^2 / m = Ohm-m.

questions.text: With an increase in temperature, the resistance of a metallic conductor:
questions.explanation: Increased thermal vibration of lattice ions increases collisions for electrons.

questions.text: The resistance of a semiconductor ________ with increase in temperature.
questions.explanation: For semiconductors, the temperature coefficient of resistance is negative because more charge carriers are released at higher temperatures.

questions.text: Drift velocity (vd) and electric field (E) are related as:
questions.explanation: vd = (eEτ / m), where τ is relaxation time.

questions.text: Current density 'j' is defined as:
questions.explanation: It is the current flowing per unit area of cross-section held normal to the direction of current.

questions.text: EMF of a cell is the potential difference between terminals when:
questions.explanation: Electromotive Force is the maximum potential difference in an open circuit.

questions.text: Internal resistance of a cell depends on:
questions.explanation: Internal resistance is caused by the opposition offered by the electrolyte to the flow of ions.

questions.text: Kirchhoff's First Law (Junction Rule) is based on the conservation of:
questions.explanation: Total current entering a junction must equal total current leaving it.

questions.text: Kirchhoff's Second Law (Loop Rule) is based on the conservation of:
questions.explanation: The algebraic sum of changes in potential around any closed loop is zero.

questions.text: A Wheatstone bridge is balanced when:
questions.explanation: Under balanced condition, no current flows through the galvanometer.

questions.text: Electric power P is given by:
questions.explanation: Power is the rate at which electrical energy is dissipated.

questions.text: Terminal potential difference V of a cell of EMF E and internal resistance r when discharging is:
questions.explanation: Potential drops across the internal resistance when current I is drawn.

questions.text: Relation between current I and drift velocity vd is:
questions.explanation: n is number density of electrons, e is charge, A is area.

questions.text: The relaxation time (τ) in a conductor ________ with increase in temperature.
questions.explanation: Collisions become more frequent at higher temperatures, reducing the time between them.

questions.text: Materials which do not obey Ohm's law are called:
questions.explanation: Examples include vacuum tubes, semi-conductor diodes, and electrolytes.

questions.text: Mobility (μ) of a charge carrier is defined as:
questions.explanation: It is the magnitude of drift velocity per unit electric field.

questions.text: SI unit of mobility is:
questions.explanation: Derived from (m/s) / (V/m) = m^2 / (Vs).

questions.text: When n identical cells each of EMF E and internal resistance r are connected in series, the total EMF is:
questions.explanation: Series connection adds up the EMFs.

questions.text: When n identical cells each of EMF E and internal resistance r are connected in parallel, the total EMF is:
questions.explanation: In parallel, the potential difference across the combination remains equal to the EMF of a single cell.

questions.text: A wire of resistance R is stretched to double its length. Its new resistance will be:
questions.explanation: When L doubles, area A halves (volume is constant). R' = ρ(2L)/(A/2) = 4ρL/A = 4R.

questions.text: Kilowatt-hour (kWh) is a unit of:
questions.explanation: 1 kWh = 3.6 * 10^6 Joules.

questions.text: The drift velocity is very small, yet electric bulbs light up almost instantly because:
options: Electric field is established at speed of light
questions.explanation: The field pushes every electron in the circuit simultaneously.

questions.text: Current is a ________ quantity.
questions.explanation: Although it has direction, it does not follow vector addition laws.

questions.text: Resistance of an ideal ammeter is:
questions.explanation: An ammeter is connected in series; zero resistance ensures it doesn't change the current in the circuit.

questions.text: Resistance of an ideal voltmeter is:
questions.explanation: Voltmeter is connected in parallel; infinite resistance ensures it draws no current from the branch.

questions.text: In a potentiometer, for a constant current, the potential drop is proportional to:
questions.explanation: V = IR = I (ρL/A). Since I, ρ, A are constant, V ∝ L.

questions.text: Which material is used to make standard resistors due to low temperature coefficient?
questions.explanation: Manganin and Constantan show very little change in resistance with temperature.

questions.text: During charging of a cell, terminal potential V is:
questions.explanation: Current flows into the positive terminal during charging.

questions.text: The resistivity of insulators is of the order of:
questions.explanation: Insulators have extremely high resistance to current flow.

questions.text: Condition of balanced Wheatstone bridge is used in:
questions.explanation: Meter bridge is a practical form of Wheatstone bridge used to measure unknown resistance.

questions.text: When cells are connected in series, the total internal resistance is:
questions.explanation: Resistance adds up in series.

questions.text: Two resistors R1 and R2 are in parallel. Equivalent resistance Rp is:
The path of electrons in a conductor in the absence of electric field is:
questions.explanation: Due to random thermal collisions with ions.

questions.text: The color code of a resistor with 5% tolerance ends with a ________ band.
questions.explanation: Gold represents 5%, Silver represents 10% tolerance.
